name: INSPIRE-SHE Scholarship
eligibility_summary: Top 1% in Class 12 board exams, pursuing science stream at UG level.
eligibility_full: Students who have secured a rank within the top 1% in their Class 12 board examinations and are pursuing courses in Basic and Natural Sciences (BSc, Integrated MSc) at any recognized institution in India.
documents_required: Class 12 mark sheet showing top 1% rank
application_process: Register on the INSPIRE web portal (online-inspire.gov.in)
Fill personal, academic and bank details
Submit application and note the application number
description: INSPIRE-SHE (Scholarship for Higher Education) is a flagship program by DST to attract talented students to pursue careers in basic and natural sciences. Provides ₹80,000 per year for up to 5 years.

name: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship
eligibility_summary: Class 9 to 12 students with family income below ₹3.5 lakh.
eligibility_full: Students of Class 9 in government, government-aided and local body schools whose parental income is not more than ₹3.5 lakh per annum. Must score at least 55% (50% for SC/ST) in Class 7 and qualify the NMMS state-level exam.
application_process: Qualify the NMMS state-level exam in Class 8
Register on the National Scholarship Portal (scholarships.gov.in)
description: Awarded to meritorious students from economically weaker sections to arrest dropout at Class 8 and encourage continuation through Class 12.

name: AICTE Pragati Scholarship for Girls
provider: All India Council for Technical Education
eligibility_summary: Girl students enrolled in AICTE-approved technical courses.
eligibility_full: Girl students admitted in the first year of AICTE-approved technical courses (degree or diploma) through centralized admission. Family income must not exceed ₹8 lakh per annum. Two girls per family are eligible.
application_process: Register on the National Scholarship Portal
Select 'AICTE Pragati Scholarship' under AICTE schemes
Fill personal, academic and bank details
Application verified by institution and AICTE
description: Aims to support girl children pursuing technical education. Covers tuition reimbursement up to ₹30,000 plus ₹2,000/month for ten months as incidental expenses.

name: Prime Minister's Research Fellowship
eligibility_summary: Direct PhD admission at IITs, IISc, NITs, IISERs or top institutes.
eligibility_full: Students with B.Tech / Integrated M.Tech / M.Sc degree from IITs, IISc, NITs, IIITs, IISERs and other top institutes. CGPA of at least 8.0 required. Selection via direct entry or lateral entry channels.
application_process: Create an account and select the application cycle
Submit research proposal and statement of purpose
Upload academic records and recommendation letters
Shortlisted candidates appear for interview at host institute
Final selection by national review committee
description: India's premier PhD scholarship, providing ₹70,000–80,000/month plus ₹2 lakh annual research grant to attract talent into research careers in cutting-edge science and technology.

name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship
eligibility_summary: Top 20% in Class 12 with family income under ₹4.5 lakh.
eligibility_full: Students above 80th percentile in Class 12 board exams, pursuing regular UG or PG courses (not distance/correspondence), with family income below ₹4.5 lakh per annum. Not availing any other scholarship.
application_process: Register on the National Scholarship Portal
Fill academic, family income, and bank details
Approval by state and Ministry of Education
description: Provides financial assistance to meritorious students from poor families to meet day-to-day expenses. Awarded for up to 4-5 years depending on the course.

name: AICTE Saksham Scholarship
provider: All India Council for Technical Education
eligibility_summary: Specially-abled students pursuing technical degree or diploma.
eligibility_full: Specially-abled students (40%+ disability as certified by competent medical authority) admitted to AICTE-approved technical degree or diploma courses. Family income must not exceed ₹8 lakh per annum.
application_process: Register on the National Scholarship Portal
Select 'AICTE Saksham Scholarship' under AICTE schemes
Upload disability certificate and academic documents
Disbursement to bank account on approval
description: Encourages specially-abled students to pursue technical education. Covers tuition fees up to ₹30,000 and ₹2,000/month for ten months as incidental expenses.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship for SC Students
eligibility_summary: SC students in post-matric courses with family income below ₹2.5 lakh.
eligibility_full: Scheduled Caste students pursuing any recognized post-matriculation course (UG, PG, professional, technical). Family income must not exceed ₹2.5 lakh per annum. Covers full tuition, maintenance allowance, study tour charges and book grant.
application_process: Register on the National Scholarship Portal
Select 'Post-Matric Scholarship for SC' under MoSJE
Fill personal, course, family and bank details
Upload caste, income, and academic documents
Verification by institution, district, and state
description: The flagship scholarship for SC students at post-matric level. Covers complete academic costs including tuition, hostel, books, and maintenance.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship for ST Students
eligibility_summary: ST students in post-matric courses with family income below ₹2.5 lakh.
eligibility_full: Scheduled Tribe students pursuing any recognized post-matric course in India. Family income from all sources must not exceed ₹2.5 lakh per annum. Covers academic costs along with maintenance allowance based on group of course.
application_process: Register on the National Scholarship Portal
Upload tribe, income, and academic documents
Verification by institution and state tribal department
description: Centrally sponsored scheme by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs to support ST students at post-matric and higher education levels.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship for OBC Students
eligibility_summary: OBC students in post-matric courses with family income below ₹1.5 lakh.
eligibility_full: Other Backward Class students pursuing any recognized post-matric course at recognized institutions. Family income from all sources must not exceed ₹1.5 lakh per annum. Covers compulsory course fees and a monthly maintenance allowance.
application_process: Register on the National Scholarship Portal
Complete the application with all required fields
Upload OBC, income, and academic documents
description: Provides financial assistance to OBC students from poor families. Monthly maintenance varies by course group from ₹230 to ₹1,200.

name: Begum Hazrat Mahal National Scholarship
eligibility_summary: Minority community girls in Class 9 to 12, family income below ₹2 lakh.
eligibility_full: Girl students from notified minority communities (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Parsi, Jain) studying in classes 9 to 12. Family income should not exceed ₹2 lakh per annum. Must have scored at least 50% in previous final exam.
application_process: Register on the National Scholarship Portal
Select 'Begum Hazrat Mahal Scholarship' under MAEF
Fill personal, family and academic details
description: Promotes education of girl children from minority communities at secondary and senior secondary level. ₹5,000 for classes 9-10 and ₹6,000 for classes 11-12.

name: UGC-NET Junior Research Fellowship
eligibility_summary: Qualified UGC NET-JRF, pursuing MPhil or PhD at recognized university.
eligibility_full: Candidates who have qualified the UGC National Eligibility Test for Junior Research Fellowship (NET-JRF) and are enrolled in MPhil or PhD programs in UGC-recognized universities and colleges.
application_process: Qualify UGC NET examination as JRF
Submit JRF award letter and joining report to university
University forwards claim to UGC every six months
description: Premier research fellowship for humanities and sciences. ₹37,000/month for first two years, ₹42,000/month thereafter, plus HRA and contingency grant. 5-year tenure.

name: National Scholarship for PwD (Post-Matric)
provider: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities
eligibility_summary: Students with 40%+ disability, family income below ₹2.5 lakh.
eligibility_full: Students with at least 40% disability as certified by a competent medical authority, pursuing any recognized post-matric course. Family income should not exceed ₹2.5 lakh per annum.
application_process: Register on the National Scholarship Portal
Upload disability certificate and other documents
Fill course and family income details
Submit for institution and district verification
Final approval and disbursement by DEPwD
description: Supports students with disabilities pursuing higher education by covering academic and maintenance expenses. Includes additional reader allowance for visually impaired students.

name: Top Class Education Scheme for SC Students
eligibility_summary: SC students admitted to 266 notified premier institutions (IITs, IIMs, NITs, etc.).
eligibility_full: Scheduled Caste students admitted to any of the 266 notified premier institutions including IITs, IIMs, NITs, NLUs, AIIMS and other top central institutions. Family income from all sources must not exceed ₹8 lakh per annum.
application_process: Secure admission to a notified premier institution
Register on the National Scholarship Portal
Select 'Top Class Education Scheme for SC'
Upload caste, income and admission documents
description: Provides full financial support to SC students admitted to top-tier institutions in India. Covers complete tuition fees, living expenses, books, and computer allowance.

name: Dr. Ambedkar Top Class Education for OBC Students
eligibility_summary: OBC students admitted to notified institutions, family income below ₹8 lakh.
eligibility_full: OBC students (non-creamy layer) admitted to notified premier institutions across India. Covers students pursuing UG, PG and professional courses. Family income should not exceed ₹8 lakh per annum.
application_process: Secure admission to notified premier institution
Register on the National Scholarship Portal
description: Provides comprehensive financial assistance to OBC students at premier institutions, covering full tuition, hostel, books and academic allowance.

name: National Overseas Scholarship for SC
eligibility_summary: SC students for overseas Masters or PhD at top universities, family income below ₹8 lakh.
eligibility_full: Scheduled Caste candidates selected for full-time Masters or PhD at top 500 globally ranked universities (QS / Times Higher Education). Age limit 35 years. Family income from all sources must not exceed ₹8 lakh per annum.
application_process: Receive admission offer from globally ranked university
description: Sponsors approximately 125 candidates per year from SC community for higher studies abroad. Covers tuition, maintenance, contingency, visa fees and one-way airfare.

name: Dr. Ambedkar Post-Matric Scholarship for EBC
eligibility_summary: Economically Backward Class students (open category), family income below ₹1 lakh.
eligibility_full: Students belonging to Economically Backward Classes (general category) pursuing post-matric courses (Class 11 onwards). Family income from all sources must not exceed ₹1 lakh per annum. Not availing any other scholarship.
application_process: Register on the National Scholarship Portal
Select 'Dr. Ambedkar Post-Matric Scholarship for EBC'
Fill family income and academic details
description: Targets economically backward students from the general/open category to support their post-matric education with tuition coverage and monthly maintenance.

name: National Fellowship for OBC (NFOBC)
provider: Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment / UGC
eligibility_summary: OBC students with UGC NET, pursuing MPhil or PhD.
eligibility_full: Other Backward Class students (non-creamy layer) who have qualified UGC NET and secured admission to MPhil/PhD program at a UGC-recognized university or institution. Provides JRF rate for first two years, SRF rate thereafter.
application_process: Submit award letter and joining report through institution
description: Provides financial support to OBC research scholars to pursue advanced research leading to MPhil and PhD degrees. ₹37,000/month for first 2 years, ₹42,000/month for next 3 years.

name: National Fellowship for SC Students
provider: Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment / UGC
eligibility_summary: SC students with UGC NET, pursuing MPhil or PhD.
eligibility_full: Scheduled Caste students with postgraduate degree and UGC NET qualification, enrolled in MPhil/PhD at UGC-recognized institutions. Formerly known as Rajiv Gandhi National Fellowship for SC. ₹37,000/month for first 2 years, ₹42,000/month thereafter.
application_process: Apply through UGC portal under NFSC
Submit award and joining documents via institution
Disbursement via institution every six months
description: Supports SC scholars pursuing advanced research. Approximately 2,000 fellowships awarded annually with full monthly stipend, HRA and contingency grant for 5-year tenure.

name: Ishan Uday Special Scholarship (Northeast)
eligibility_summary: Students from Northeast region pursuing UG, family income below ₹4.5 lakh.
eligibility_full: Students who are permanent residents of any of the 8 Northeast states (Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Sikkim, Tripura), passed Class 12 from a Northeast board, and secured admission to General/Technical/Medical/Professional UG course. Family income should not exceed ₹4.5 lakh per annum.
documents_required: Class 12 mark sheet from NE board
application_process: Apply on UGC Ishan Uday portal
Fill personal, academic and financial details
description: Targets students from Northeast India to encourage higher education by providing 10,000 fresh scholarships annually. ₹5,400/month for technical and ₹3,200 for general degree courses.

name: PM Scholarship Scheme for Wards of Ex-Servicemen
provider: Department of Ex-Servicemen Welfare, Ministry of Defence
eligibility_summary: Wards or widows of ex-servicemen pursuing professional UG courses.
eligibility_full: Wards/widows of ex-servicemen and ex-Coast Guards (below officer rank), wards of police personnel killed in terrorist/Naxal attacks, pursuing professional degree courses (Engineering, MBBS, BDS, Veterinary, BBA, BCA, MBA, MCA, B.Pharma, B.Ed, B.Sc Agriculture etc.). Must have scored at least 60% in Class 12 or equivalent.
documents_required: Ex-servicemen ID card / pension book / discharge book
application_process: Register on the National Scholarship Portal (KSB section)
Fill personal, defence and academic details
description: Promotes higher technical and professional education among wards of ex-servicemen. 5,500 fresh scholarships awarded yearly through Kendriya Sainik Board.

name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for Minorities (Class 9-10)
eligibility_summary: Minority students in Class 9 and 10 with family income below ₹1 lakh.
eligibility_full: Students from notified minority communities (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Parsi, Jain) studying in Class 9 and 10 at any government or recognized private school. Family income should not exceed ₹1 lakh per annum. Must have scored at least 50% in previous final exam.
application_process: Register on the National Scholarship Portal
Fill personal, family and academic details
description: Encourages parents from minority communities to send their children to school by providing financial assistance to cover educational expenses at Class 9 and 10 level.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship for Minorities
eligibility_summary: Minority students from Class 11 to PhD with family income below ₹2 lakh.
eligibility_full: Students from notified minority communities pursuing courses from Class 11 to PhD level at any recognized institution. Family income should not exceed ₹2 lakh per annum. Must have scored at least 50% in previous final exam.
application_process: Register on the National Scholarship Portal
description: Provides financial support to minority students pursuing higher and technical education to enable them to complete their studies without financial burden.

name: Merit-cum-Means Scholarship for Minorities
eligibility_summary: Minority students in professional/technical courses, family income below ₹2.5 lakh.
eligibility_full: Students from notified minority communities pursuing professional and technical courses (Engineering, MBBS, MBA, etc.) at recognized institutions. Family income should not exceed ₹2.5 lakh per annum. Must have scored at least 50% in qualifying exam.
application_process: Register on the National Scholarship Portal
Fill personal, course and family details
description: Aims at financial assistance to poor and meritorious students from minority communities so they can pursue professional and technical courses. 60,000 fresh scholarships awarded annually.

name: CBSE Merit Scholarship for Single Girl Child
eligibility_summary: Single girl child scoring 60%+ in Class 10 CBSE, tuition fee below ₹1,500/month.
eligibility_full: Single girl child of her parents who has appeared for Class 10 CBSE board exam with at least 60% (6.2 CGPA), pursuing Class 11 in a CBSE-affiliated school. Tuition fee should not exceed ₹1,500 per month at the time of admission to Class 11.
documents_required: Single girl child affidavit by parents (notarized)
application_process: Register and fill the application form
Upload single girl child affidavit and academic documents
Renewal in Class 12 based on Class 11 performance
description: Encourages parents to educate their single girl child and to provide financial assistance to meritorious single girl children for their continuing education at senior secondary stage.

name: National Fellowship for Persons with Disabilities
provider: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities / UGC
eligibility_summary: PwD students with UGC NET, pursuing MPhil or PhD.
eligibility_full: Students with at least 40% disability as certified by a competent medical authority, having postgraduate degree and UGC NET qualification, enrolled in MPhil/PhD program at UGC-recognized institutions.
application_process: Fellowship disbursed through institution every six months
description: Supports research scholars with disabilities. Provides JRF rate for first 2 years and SRF rate for next 3 years, along with HRA and contingency. Approximately 200 fellowships annually.

name: Rajarshi Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj EBC Tuition Fee Scholarship
eligibility_summary: Maharashtra domicile, open category EBC, family income below ₹8 lakh, in professional courses.
eligibility_full: Open category (non-reserved) students from Maharashtra pursuing professional courses (Engineering, Medical, Pharmacy, Architecture, MBA, etc.). Family income from all sources should not exceed ₹8 lakh per annum. Maximum two children per family eligible. Minimum 50% attendance required (except first semester).
documents_required: Admission letter through CAP / centralized admission
application_process: Select 'Rajarshi Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj EBC Yojna'
Fill personal, academic and family income details
Benefits credited in 2 installments to Aadhaar-linked bank account
description: Flagship Maharashtra scholarship covering 100% tuition and exam fees for open category EBC students in professional courses. Implemented through the MahaDBT portal across DHE, DTE and other departments.

name: Dr. Punjabrao Deshmukh Vasatigruh Nirvah Bhatta Yojna
eligibility_summary: Maharashtra hostel residents, family income below ₹8 lakh (professional) or ₹1 lakh (non-professional).
eligibility_full: Open category students from Maharashtra living in college/private hostel while pursuing higher education. Quota for children of small landholder farmers, registered labour and economically weaker section students. Family income ceiling: ₹8L for professional courses, ₹1L for non-professional.
application_process: Select 'Dr. Panjabrao Deshmukh Vasatigruh Nirvah Bhatta Yojna'
description: Maintenance allowance to support hostel-residing students from economically weaker open category. Available under multiple departments (DHE, DTE, DOA, MAFSU, AGR).

name: Tuition Fee & Exam Fee Freeship for Tribal Students
provider: Tribal Development Department, Government of Maharashtra
eligibility_summary: Maharashtra ST students, family income below ₹2.5 lakh.
eligibility_full: Scheduled Tribe students who are permanent residents of Maharashtra and pursuing any recognized post-matric course. Family income from all sources should not exceed ₹2.5 lakh per annum. Available for students who are not eligible for Central Government Post-Matric Scholarship.
application_process: Select 'Tuition Fee & Exam Fee for Tribal Students (Freeship)'
Fill personal, tribal and academic details
Submit for institutional and Project Officer verification
description: Provides full tuition and examination fee waiver for tribal students in Maharashtra not covered by the central Post-Matric Scholarship, ensuring no tribal student drops out due to fees.

name: Rajarshi Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj Merit Scholarship (VJNT/SBC)
eligibility_summary: VJNT/SBC students in Class 11 and 12, Maharashtra domicile.
eligibility_full: Students from Vimukta Jati, Nomadic Tribes (VJNT) and Special Backward Class (SBC) categories studying in Class 11 and 12 in Maharashtra. Must have scored 50%+ in Class 10 board exam. Maharashtra domicile required.
application_process: Select 'Rajarshi Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj Merit Scholarship for 11th and 12th VJNT/SBC'
description: Merit-based monthly scholarship to encourage VJNT and SBC students in Maharashtra to continue education at higher secondary level.

name: Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar National Research Fellowship (BANRF)
provider: Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Research and Training Institute (BARTI), Pune
eligibility_summary: SC students from Maharashtra pursuing PhD, must qualify BARTI CET.
eligibility_full: Scheduled Caste students who are permanent residents of Maharashtra, registered for PhD at a recognized university. Selection is through the BARTI Common Entrance Test (CET). ₹31,000/month for first 2 years, ₹35,000/month for next 3 years, plus HRA and ₹10,000-20,000 annual contingency.
application_process: Apply for the BARTI CET online (barticet.in)
Submit detailed application with documents if shortlisted
Award letter issued; fellowship disbursed via institution
description: Maharashtra's flagship PhD fellowship for SC scholars, administered by BARTI Pune. Modeled on the UGC fellowship structure, it has supported thousands of SC research scholars since 2013.

name: Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj National Research Fellowship (CSMNRF)
provider: SARTHI - Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj Research, Training and Human Development Institute, Pune
eligibility_summary: Maratha, Kunbi, Maratha-Kunbi students from Maharashtra pursuing MPhil or PhD.
eligibility_full: Students from Maratha, Kunbi, Maratha-Kunbi or Kunbi-Maratha communities, permanent residents of Maharashtra, registered for MPhil or PhD at a recognized Indian university. Family income from all sources should not exceed ₹8 lakh. Non-creamy layer required. 50% reserved for women, 5% for PwD.
application_process: Apply online on the SARTHI portal (eform.sarthi-maharashtragov.in)
Submit printed application with documents to SARTHI office
Selection through SARTHI CET if applicable
description: SARTHI's flagship research fellowship for the Maratha-Kunbi community, structured similarly to UGC's JRF/SRF. Supports MPhil (2 years) and PhD (5 years) scholars.

name: MAHAJYOTI Research Fellowship for OBC/VJNT/SBC PhD
provider: Mahatma Jyotiba Phule Research and Training Institute (MAHAJYOTI), Nagpur
eligibility_summary: OBC, VJNT or SBC students from Maharashtra pursuing PhD.
eligibility_full: Students from OBC, VJNT (Vimukta Jati Nomadic Tribes), or SBC (Special Backward Class) categories who are permanent residents of Maharashtra and registered for PhD at a recognized university. Non-creamy layer certificate required for OBC.
application_process: Apply online on the MAHAJYOTI portal (mahajyoti.org.in)
Submit application along with required documents
description: MAHAJYOTI's PhD fellowship for OBC, VJNT and SBC research scholars from Maharashtra. Established to provide equitable research opportunities, equivalent in structure to BARTI and SARTHI fellowships.

name: TRTI Research Fellowship for ST PhD Students
provider: Tribal Research and Training Institute (TRTI), Pune
eligibility_summary: Scheduled Tribe students from Maharashtra pursuing PhD.
eligibility_full: Scheduled Tribe students who are permanent residents of Maharashtra and registered for PhD at a recognized university or research institution. Selection through TRTI entrance test where applicable.
application_process: Apply online on the TRTI portal (barticet.in/TRTI)
Appear for TRTI entrance test if applicable
description: TRTI's PhD fellowship for tribal research scholars from Maharashtra. Parallel to BARTI/SARTHI/MAHAJYOTI fellowships for other communities.

name: Dr. Punjabrao Deshmukh SARATHI Higher Education Scholarship
eligibility_summary: Maratha-Kunbi students from Maharashtra in select top 200 colleges.
eligibility_full: Students from Maratha, Kunbi, Maratha-Kunbi or Kunbi-Maratha communities residing in Maharashtra, admitted through entrance examination (not management quota) to one of 200 selected colleges across India. Family income should not exceed ₹8 lakh per annum. Minimum 55% required in qualifying exam.
application_process: Verify your college is in the SARTHI-approved list
Apply online on the SARTHI portal
Submit printed application with documents to SARTHI office
Disbursement of tuition directly to institution
description: SARTHI's UG/PG scholarship covering full tuition and exam fees for 300 Maratha-Kunbi students per year admitted to 200 selected top institutions across India.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship for OBC Girls in Professional Courses
eligibility_summary: OBC girls from Maharashtra in professional courses, family income below ₹8 lakh.
eligibility_full: Girl students belonging to OBC category, permanent residents of Maharashtra, admitted to professional courses (Engineering, Medical, Pharmacy, MBA, etc.) through centralized admission. Family income from all sources should not exceed ₹8 lakh per annum. Non-creamy layer required.
documents_required: Admission letter through CAP / centralized admission
application_process: Select 'Post Matric Scholarship to OBC Girls in Professional Courses'
Fill personal, academic and family income details
Approval by VJNT/OBC department and disbursement via DBT
description: Targeted scholarship for OBC girl students in Maharashtra pursuing professional and technical degrees, covering full tuition and providing monthly maintenance allowance.
